---
title: "ADHDCYCLE_6_subgroup_hormonecorr"
author: "Tory Eisenlohr-Moul"
date: "2026-03-13"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup}
# This chunk sets the global options for the entire R Markdown document.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
options(scipen = 999, digits = 3)
```

# Load Required Libraries

```{r libraries}
# This chunk loads all the packages needed for the analysis.
library(tidyverse)       # A collection of essential packages for data manipulation and visualization (includes dplyr, ggplot2, etc.)
library(janitor)         # Provides functions for cleaning data and table creation.
library(haven)           # For reading data from other statistical software like SPSS (.sav).
library(readxl)          # For reading data from Excel files (.xls, .xlsx).
library(zoo)             # For calculating rolling averages.
library(lubridate)       # For making it easier to work with dates and times.
library(conflicted)      # Helps manage function name conflicts between packages.
#remotes::install_github("eisenlohrmoullab/menstrualcycleR")
library(menstrualcycleR) # For cycle-specific analyses like PACTS scaling.
library(gridExtra)       # For arranging multiple plots on one page.
library(ggvenn)          # For creating Venn diagrams.
library(readr)       # For reading CSV files
library(dplyr)       # For data wrangling
library(lubridate)   # For date parsing/manipulation
library(tidyr)       # For tidying data
library(rlang)       # For tidy evaluation
library(visdat)      # For missing data visualization
library(ggpubr)      # For publication-ready plots
library(tidyverse) 
library(mgcv)      
library(readr)     
library(knitr)     
library(lme4)
library(gratia)
library(patchwork)
library(rmcorr)
# Explicitly state our function preferences to avoid ambiguity when multiple
# packages have a function with the same name.
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("lag", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("summarize", "dplyr")
```


# --- Output Folder ---

```{r define_box_output}

# Define a base output location and create a unique, timestamped subfolder for this specific run.
output_folder_base <- "~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_code_dataedits_output"
output_folder <- file.path(output_folder_base, format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d_Run"))
if (!dir.exists(output_folder)) {
  dir.create(output_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}
```


# OLD CODE, FULL SAMPLE CORRS -- ICCs, Between- and Within-Person Associations of Study Variables
```{r f1_corr, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ===================================================================
# 1. Load Required Libraries
# ===================================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(rmcorr)
library(performance)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)

# ===================================================================
# 2. Define the Heatmap Generation Function (Final Version)
# ===================================================================
generate_correlation_heatmap <- function(
    data, outcome_vars, id_var, final_labels = NULL, # <-- New argument
    sig.level = 0.05,
    highlight_diag = TRUE,
    show_legend = TRUE,
    label_size = 4.6,
    diag_label_size = 6,
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Within-person (rmcorr); Lower: Between-person (Spearman); Diagonal: ICC"
) {
  # (The first part of the function is the same as the last version)
  n_vars <- length(outcome_vars)
  results_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_vars, ncol = n_vars,
                           dimnames = list(outcome_vars, outcome_vars))
  pval_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_vars, ncol = n_vars,
                        dimnames = list(outcome_vars, outcome_vars))
  
  summary_expressions <- purrr::map(outcome_vars, ~ rlang::quo(mean(!!rlang::sym(.x), na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
    rlang::set_names(outcome_vars)
    
  person_means <- data %>%
    group_by(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    summarise(!!!summary_expressions, .groups = "drop")

  between_corr <- suppressWarnings(
    cor(person_means[, outcome_vars], method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )

  for (i in 1:(n_vars - 1)) for (j in (i + 1):n_vars) {
    x <- person_means[[outcome_vars[i]]]
    y <- person_means[[outcome_vars[j]]]
    if (sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)) > 2) {
      test <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman"))
      results_matrix[i, j] <- between_corr[i, j]
      pval_matrix[i, j] <- test$p.value
    }
  }

  for (i in 1:n_vars) {
    var <- outcome_vars[i]
    fml <- as.formula(paste0("`", var, "` ~ 1 + (1|`", id_var, "`)"))
    if (sum(!is.na(data[[var]])) > 1) {
      model_fit <- try(lmer(fml, data = data), silent = TRUE)
      if (!inherits(model_fit, "try-error")) {
        icc_val <- performance::icc(model_fit)$ICC_adjusted
        results_matrix[i, i] <- icc_val
        pval_matrix[i, i] <- 0
      }
    }
  }

  for (i in 2:n_vars) for (j in 1:(i - 1)) {
    var1 <- outcome_vars[i]; var2 <- outcome_vars[j]
    rmcorr_data <- data %>% select(all_of(c(id_var, var1, var2))) %>% drop_na()
    if (nrow(rmcorr_data) > 2) {
      rmcorr_result <- rmcorr::rmcorr(participant = id_var, measure1 = var1, measure2 = var2, dataset = rmcorr_data)
      results_matrix[i, j] <- rmcorr_result$r
      pval_matrix[i, j] <- rmcorr_result$p
    }
  }

  within_corr_matrix <- results_matrix
  within_corr_matrix[upper.tri(within_corr_matrix, diag = TRUE)] <- 0
  dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - abs(within_corr_matrix))
  hclust_obj <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D2")
  corr_order <- rownames(within_corr_matrix)[hclust_obj$order]

  results_matrix_ordered <- results_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]
  pval_matrix_ordered    <- pval_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix_ordered))
  names(plot_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "value")
  plot_df$p.value <- as.vector(pval_matrix_ordered)
  plot_df$Var1 <- factor(plot_df$Var1, levels = rev(corr_order))
  plot_df$Var2 <- factor(plot_df$Var2, levels = corr_order)

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(is.na(x), NA_character_, sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x)))
  }

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      is_diag = as.character(Var1) == as.character(Var2),
      is_sig  = p.value < sig.level,
      label   = no_leading_zero(value)
    )

  diag_df    <- plot_df %>% filter(is_diag)
  sig_df     <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & is_sig)
  nonsig_df  <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & !is_sig)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_tile(data = plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag),
              aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value), color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_tile(data = diag_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
              fill = "gray92", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#6D9EC1", mid = "white", high = "#E46726",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Correlation") +
    labs(title = "Multilevel Correlation Matrix of Daily Outcomes", subtitle = subtitle_text) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 20),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 12, margin = margin(b = 6)),
      axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y   = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title    = element_blank(),
      legend.position = if (show_legend) "right" else "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_blank()
    )

  p <- p +
    geom_text(data = sig_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
              fontface = "bold", color = "black", size = label_size, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = nonsig_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
              color = "gray40", size = label_size, na.rm = TRUE)

  if (highlight_diag && nrow(diag_df) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_text(data = diag_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
                fontface = "bold", color = "#4B0082", size = diag_label_size, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  # === NEW CODE TO RELABEL AXES AT THE END ===
  if (!is.null(final_labels)) {
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = final_labels) +
      scale_y_discrete(labels = final_labels)
  }
  # ===========================================

  return(p)
}

# ===================================================================
# 3. Define Parameters and Run the Plot
# ===================================================================
# Technical names mapped to the desired "pretty" labels
corrplotlist <- c(
  "CSS_Inatt" = "Inattentive Sx",
  "CSS_HypImp" = "Hyperactive/Impulsive Sx",
  "E2.3roll" = "E2",
  "P4.3roll" = "P4",
  "score_pinball_rev" = "Working Memory Deficits (Pinball Task)",
  "score_robot_rev" = "Response Disinhibition (Robot Task)",
  "DRSP_1" = "Depression",
  "DRSP_4" = "Anxiety",
  "DRSP_7" = "Irritability",
  "DRSP_21" = "Physical Pain"
)

# This assumes `df` exists in your environment
if (exists("df") && exists("corrplotlist")) {

  # === NEW: Create safe labels for calculation and a lookup table for plotting ===
  # 1. Create safe labels (e.g., "Hyperactive.Impulsive.Sx") for calculations
  safe_labels <- make.names(corrplotlist)
  
  # 2. Create a lookup table to map safe labels back to pretty labels
  label_lookup <- setNames(corrplotlist, safe_labels)
  # ===============================================================================

  # Prepare the data by renaming columns to the SAFE labels
  original_vars <- names(corrplotlist)
  plot_data <- df[, c("id", original_vars)]
  names(plot_data)[match(original_vars, names(plot_data))] <- safe_labels # Use safe_labels here

  # Call the function with the safe labels for calculation AND the lookup table for the final plot
  correlation_heatmap <- generate_correlation_heatmap(
    data = plot_data,
    outcome_vars = safe_labels, # Use safe_labels here
    id_var = "id",
    final_labels = label_lookup # Pass the lookup table for final relabeling
  )

  print(correlation_heatmap)

  # Define the final filename, including today's date
dated_output_file <- file.path(output_folder, 
                               paste0("corr_fullsample", 
                                      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), 
                                      ".png"))

# Save the plot directly to the final, dated file
ggsave(filename = dated_output_file, 
       plot = correlation_heatmap,
       width = 11,
       height = 8.5)

} else {
  
  stop("Error: 'df' or 'corrplotlist' not found in the environment.")
}
```






# adding correlation annotations

```{r}
# ===================================================================
# 1. Load Required Libraries
# ===================================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(rmcorr)
library(performance)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)

# ===================================================================
# 2. Load participant-level GAM significance summary
# ===================================================================
singlegaminfo <- read_csv(
  "/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_code_dataedits_output/5_singlegams_output/20250923_Participant_Reports_PDF_byID/participant_significance_summary_20250923.csv"
)

# ===================================================================
# 3. Harmonize ID type and create participant-level significance flags
# ===================================================================
df <- df %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))

participant_significance <- singlegaminfo %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id)) %>%
  filter(variable %in% c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp")) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_flag = p.value < 0.05,
    variable = recode(
      variable,
      "CSS_Inatt" = "significant_inatt",
      "CSS_HypImp" = "significant_hypimp"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(id, variable, sig_flag) %>%
  group_by(id, variable) %>%
  summarise(sig_flag = any(sig_flag, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = sig_flag,
    values_fill = list(sig_flag = FALSE)
  )

df_with_significance <- df %>%
  left_join(participant_significance, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    significant_inatt = replace_na(significant_inatt, FALSE),
    significant_hypimp = replace_na(significant_hypimp, FALSE)
  )

# ===================================================================
# 4. Add person-standardized hormone variables
#    ONLY for within-person use later
# ===================================================================
df_with_significance <- df_with_significance %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    E2_mean = mean(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_mean = mean(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_sd = sd(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_sd = sd(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_pz = case_when(
      is.na(E2_sd) | E2_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (E2 - E2_mean) / E2_sd
    ),
    P4_pz = case_when(
      is.na(P4_sd) | P4_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (P4 - P4_mean) / P4_sd
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-E2_mean, -P4_mean, -E2_sd, -P4_sd)

# ===================================================================
# 5. Create subgroup datasets
# ===================================================================
sig_inatt_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt)

sig_hypimp_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_hypimp)

sig_both_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

sig_either_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt | significant_hypimp)

inatt_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & !significant_hypimp)

hypimp_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(!significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

group_counts <- tibble(
  group = c(
    "sig_inatt",
    "sig_hypimp",
    "sig_both",
    "sig_either",
    "inatt_only",
    "hypimp_only"
  ),
  n_people = c(
    n_distinct(sig_inatt_df$id),
    n_distinct(sig_hypimp_df$id),
    n_distinct(sig_both_df$id),
    n_distinct(sig_either_df$id),
    n_distinct(inatt_only_df$id),
    n_distinct(hypimp_only_df$id)
  )
)

print(group_counts)

write_csv(
  group_counts,
  file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_group_counts_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 6. Variable specification for hybrid matrix
#    Display labels stay simple: E2 / P4
#    Between-person + ICC use RAW E2 / P4
#    Within-person uses PERSON-STANDARDIZED E2_pz / P4_pz
# ===================================================================
corr_var_info <- tibble(
  display_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  display_label = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "Inattentive Sx",
    "Hyperactive/Impulsive Sx",
    "Working Memory Deficits (Pinball Task)",
    "Response Disinhibition (Robot Task)",
    "Depression",
    "Anxiety",
    "Irritability",
    "Physical Pain"
  ),
  between_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  within_var = c(
    "E2_pz",
    "P4_pz",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 7. Helper to extract ICC robustly
# ===================================================================
extract_icc_value <- function(icc_out) {

  if (is.null(icc_out)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && !is.null(names(icc_out))) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_adjusted"]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_conditional"]]))
    }
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && is.numeric(icc_out)) {
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.data.frame(icc_out)) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  if (is.list(icc_out)) {
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_adjusted)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_conditional)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  NA_real_
}

# ===================================================================
# 8. Compute hybrid multilevel correlation matrices
# ===================================================================
compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid <- function(data, var_info, id_var) {

  display_vars <- var_info$display_var
  n_vars <- length(display_vars)

  results_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  pval_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Upper triangle = between-person Spearman on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  between_summary_expressions <- purrr::map(
    var_info$between_var,
    ~ rlang::quo(mean(!!rlang::sym(.x), na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
    rlang::set_names(display_vars)

  person_means <- data %>%
    group_by(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    summarise(!!!between_summary_expressions, .groups = "drop")

  if (nrow(person_means) >= 3) {
    between_corr <- suppressWarnings(
      cor(
        person_means[, display_vars],
        method = "spearman",
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
      )
    )

    for (i in 1:(n_vars - 1)) {
      for (j in (i + 1):n_vars) {
        x <- person_means[[display_vars[i]]]
        y <- person_means[[display_vars[j]]]

        if (sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)) > 2) {
          test <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman"))
          results_matrix[i, j] <- between_corr[i, j]
          pval_matrix[i, j] <- test$p.value
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Diagonal = ICC on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in seq_len(n_vars)) {
    raw_var <- var_info$between_var[i]
    display_var <- var_info$display_var[i]

    fml <- as.formula(paste0("`", raw_var, "` ~ 1 + (1|`", id_var, "`)"))

    if (sum(!is.na(data[[raw_var]])) > 1 &&
        dplyr::n_distinct(data[[id_var]][!is.na(data[[raw_var]])]) > 1) {

      model_fit <- try(
        suppressWarnings(lmer(fml, data = data)),
        silent = TRUE
      )

      if (!inherits(model_fit, "try-error")) {
        icc_out <- try(
          suppressWarnings(performance::icc(model_fit)),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(icc_out, "try-error")) {
          icc_val <- extract_icc_value(icc_out)

          if (is.finite(icc_val)) {
            results_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- icc_val
            pval_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- 0
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Lower triangle = within-person rmcorr on person-standardized hormones
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in 2:n_vars) {
    for (j in 1:(i - 1)) {

      var1_display <- display_vars[i]
      var2_display <- display_vars[j]

      var1_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var1_display]
      var2_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var2_display]

      rmcorr_data <- data %>%
        select(
          all_of(id_var),
          x = all_of(var1_within),
          y = all_of(var2_within)
        ) %>%
        drop_na()

      if (nrow(rmcorr_data) > 2 && n_distinct(rmcorr_data[[id_var]]) > 1) {
        rmcorr_result <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id_var,
            measure1 = x,
            measure2 = y,
            dataset = rmcorr_data
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(rmcorr_result, "try-error")) {
          results_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$r
          pval_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$p
        }
      }
    }
  }

  list(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    person_means = person_means
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 9. Convert matrices to long table
# ===================================================================
matrices_to_table <- function(results_matrix, pval_matrix, label_lookup, group_label, n_people) {

  table_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix)) %>%
    rename(var_row = Var1, var_col = Var2, estimate = Freq) %>%
    mutate(
      p_value = as.vector(pval_matrix),
      row_index = match(var_row, rownames(results_matrix)),
      col_index = match(var_col, colnames(results_matrix)),
      cell_type = case_when(
        var_row == var_col ~ "ICC",
        row_index < col_index ~ "Between-person Spearman",
        row_index > col_index ~ "Within-person rmcorr",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      var_row_label = label_lookup[var_row],
      var_col_label = label_lookup[var_col],
      group = group_label,
      n_people = n_people
    ) %>%
    select(group, n_people, cell_type, var_row, var_row_label, var_col, var_col_label, estimate, p_value)

  table_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 10. Heatmap function
#     Thick outlines highlight WITHIN-person hormone cells
# ===================================================================
generate_correlation_heatmap <- function(
    results_matrix,
    pval_matrix,
    final_labels = NULL,
    main_title = "Multilevel Correlation Matrix of Daily Outcomes",
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)",
    sig.level = 0.05,
    label_size = 4.6,
    diag_label_size = 6
) {

  corr_order <- rownames(results_matrix)

  results_matrix_ordered <- results_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]
  pval_matrix_ordered <- pval_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix_ordered))
  names(plot_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "value")
  plot_df$p.value <- as.vector(pval_matrix_ordered)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      row_mat = match(as.character(Var1), corr_order),
      col_mat = match(as.character(Var2), corr_order),
      is_diag = row_mat == col_mat,
      within_person = row_mat > col_mat,
      between_person = row_mat < col_mat
    )

  plot_df$Var1 <- factor(plot_df$Var1, levels = rev(corr_order))
  plot_df$Var2 <- factor(plot_df$Var2, levels = corr_order)

  highlight_df <- plot_df %>%
    filter(
      within_person,
      as.character(Var1) %in% c("E2", "P4") | as.character(Var2) %in% c("E2", "P4")
    )

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      label = no_leading_zero(value),
      is_sig = p.value < sig.level
    )

  diag_df <- plot_df %>% filter(is_diag)
  sig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & is_sig)
  nonsig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & !is_sig)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_tile(
      data = plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag),
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value),
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = "gray92",
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = highlight_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = NA,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 2
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#6D9EC1",
      mid = "white",
      high = "#E46726",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits = c(-1, 1),
      name = "Correlation"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = main_title,
      subtitle = subtitle_text
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank()
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = sig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "black",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = nonsig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      color = "gray40",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "#4B0082",
      size = diag_label_size
    )

  if (!is.null(final_labels)) {
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = final_labels) +
      scale_y_discrete(labels = final_labels)
  }

  return(p)
}

# ===================================================================
# 11. Wrapper
# ===================================================================
run_and_save_correlation_outputs <- function(
    data,
    group_label,
    corr_var_info,
    output_folder,
    id_var = "id"
) {

  n_people <- data %>%
    distinct(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    nrow()

  message("Running group: ", group_label, " (N = ", n_people, ")")

  if (n_people < 3) {
    warning("Skipping ", group_label, ": fewer than 3 participants.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  label_lookup <- setNames(corr_var_info$display_label, corr_var_info$display_var)

  matrix_results <- compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid(
    data = data,
    var_info = corr_var_info,
    id_var = id_var
  )

  results_matrix <- matrix_results$results_matrix
  pval_matrix <- matrix_results$pval_matrix

  corr_table <- matrices_to_table(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    label_lookup = label_lookup,
    group_label = group_label,
    n_people = n_people
  )

  dated_csv_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_table_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )

  write_csv(corr_table, dated_csv_file)

  heatmap_title <- paste0(
    "Multilevel Correlation Matrix: ",
    group_label,
    " (N = ",
    n_people,
    ")"
  )

  heatmap_plot <- generate_correlation_heatmap(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    final_labels = label_lookup,
    main_title = heatmap_title,
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)"
  )

  dated_png_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_heatmap_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".png")
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = dated_png_file,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    width = 11,
    height = 8.5
  )

  print(heatmap_plot)

  list(
    n_people = n_people,
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    corr_table = corr_table,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    csv_file = dated_csv_file,
    png_file = dated_png_file
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 12. Run subgroup outputs
# ===================================================================
sig_inatt_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = sig_inatt_df,
  group_label = "sig_inatt",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

sig_hypimp_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = sig_hypimp_df,
  group_label = "sig_hypimp",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

sig_both_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = sig_both_df,
  group_label = "sig_both",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

sig_either_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = sig_either_df,
  group_label = "sig_either",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)
# ===================================================================
# 12. Person-standardize E2 and P4 within participant
#     These are within-person z-scores
# ===================================================================
df_plotmods <- df_plotmods %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    E2_mean = mean(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_mean = mean(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_sd = sd(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_sd = sd(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_pz = case_when(
      is.na(E2_sd) | E2_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (E2 - E2_mean) / E2_sd
    ),
    P4_pz = case_when(
      is.na(P4_sd) | P4_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (P4 - P4_mean) / P4_sd
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-E2_mean, -P4_mean, -E2_sd, -P4_sd)

# ===================================================================
# 13. Helper function to prepare scatterplot data
# ===================================================================
prepare_moderation_scatter_data <- function(data, subgroup_var) {

  plot_df <- data %>%
    select(
      id,
      all_of(subgroup_var),
      E2_pz,
      P4_pz,
      CSS_Inatt,
      CSS_HypImp
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(E2_pz, P4_pz),
      names_to = "hormone",
      values_to = "hormone_std"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(CSS_Inatt, CSS_HypImp),
      names_to = "outcome",
      values_to = "symptom"
    ) %>%
    drop_na(hormone_std, symptom)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      subgroup_status = .data[[subgroup_var]],
      subgroup_status = factor(subgroup_status, levels = c("Not in subgroup", "In subgroup")),
      hormone = factor(
        hormone,
        levels = c("E2_pz", "P4_pz"),
        labels = c("Person-standardized E2", "Person-standardized P4")
      ),
      outcome = factor(
        outcome,
        levels = c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp"),
        labels = c("Daily Inattentive Symptoms", "Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms")
      ),
      panel = factor(
        paste(hormone, outcome, sep = " -> "),
        levels = c(
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms"
        )
      )
    )

  plot_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 14. Helper function to count people in and out of subgroup
# ===================================================================
get_subgroup_counts <- function(data, subgroup_var) {
  data %>%
    distinct(id, .data[[subgroup_var]]) %>%
    count(.data[[subgroup_var]], name = "n_people") %>%
    complete(
      !!rlang::sym(subgroup_var) := c("Not in subgroup", "In subgroup"),
      fill = list(n_people = 0)
    ) %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(.data[[subgroup_var]], ": N = ", n_people))
}

# ===================================================================
# 15. Helper function to compute within-person rmcorr labels per panel/group
# ===================================================================
compute_panel_rmcorr_labels <- function(plot_df) {

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  combos <- plot_df %>%
    distinct(panel, subgroup_status, hormone, outcome)

  label_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
    list(combos$panel, combos$subgroup_status, combos$hormone, combos$outcome),
    function(panel_i, subgroup_i, hormone_i, outcome_i) {

      dat_i <- plot_df %>%
        filter(
          panel == panel_i,
          subgroup_status == subgroup_i,
          hormone == hormone_i,
          outcome == outcome_i
        ) %>%
        select(id, hormone_std, symptom) %>%
        drop_na()

      n_people <- n_distinct(dat_i$id)

      if (nrow(dat_i) < 3 || n_people < 2) {
        label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
      } else {
        rmc <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id,
            measure1 = hormone_std,
            measure2 = symptom,
            dataset = dat_i
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (inherits(rmc, "try-error")) {
          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
        } else {
          r_txt <- no_leading_zero(rmc$r)

          stars <- case_when(
            rmc$p < .001 ~ "***",
            rmc$p < .01 ~ "**",
            rmc$p < .05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
          )

          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = ", r_txt, stars)
        }
      }

      tibble(
        panel = panel_i,
        subgroup_status = subgroup_i,
        label = label_text,
        x = Inf,
        y = Inf,
        vjust = ifelse(subgroup_i == "In subgroup", 1.2, 2.6)
      )
    }
  )

  label_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 16. Plotting function
# ===================================================================
plot_hormone_symptom_moderation <- function(
    data,
    subgroup_var,
    subgroup_label,
    output_folder,
    point_alpha = 0.18,
    point_size = 1.2,
    line_size = 1.1
) {

  plot_df <- prepare_moderation_scatter_data(data, subgroup_var)
  subgroup_counts <- get_subgroup_counts(data, subgroup_var)
  label_df <- compute_panel_rmcorr_labels(plot_df)

  count_text <- paste(subgroup_counts$label, collapse = "    |    ")

  p <- ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(x = hormone_std, y = symptom, color = subgroup_status)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = point_alpha, size = point_size) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = line_size) +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = subgroup_status, vjust = vjust),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 1.05,
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ panel,
      nrow = 2,
      scales = "free"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Hormone-Symptom Associations Moderated by ", subgroup_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        count_text,
        "\nText in each panel shows within-person repeated-measures correlation (rmcorr)."
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Daily symptom level",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0(
      "scatter_moderation_personstandardized_",
      subgroup_var,
      "_",
      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"),
      ".png"
    )
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 8
  )

  invisible(
    list(
      plot = p,
      plot_data = plot_df,
      label_data = label_df,
      output_file = output_file
    )
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 17. Run moderation scatterplots for subgroup definitions
# ===================================================================
scatter_sig_inatt_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "subgroup_sig_inatt",
  subgroup_label = "Significant Inattentive GAM",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_sig_hypimp_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "subgroup_sig_hypimp",
  subgroup_label = "Significant Hyperactive/Impulsive GAM",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_sig_both_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "subgroup_sig_both",
  subgroup_label = "Significant on Both GAMs",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_sig_either_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "subgroup_sig_either",
  subgroup_label = "Significant on Either GAM",
  output_folder = output_folder
)
```
```{r}
# ===================================================================
# 1. Load Required Libraries
# ===================================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(rmcorr)
library(performance)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)

# ===================================================================
# 2. Load participant-level GAM significance summary
# ===================================================================
singlegaminfo <- read_csv(
  "/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_code_dataedits_output/5_singlegams_output/20250923_Participant_Reports_PDF_byID/participant_significance_summary_20250923.csv"
)

# ===================================================================
# 3. Harmonize ID type and create participant-level cyclical flags
# ===================================================================
df <- df %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))

participant_significance <- singlegaminfo %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id)) %>%
  filter(variable %in% c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp")) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_flag = p.value < 0.05,
    variable = recode(
      variable,
      "CSS_Inatt" = "significant_inatt",
      "CSS_HypImp" = "significant_hypimp"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(id, variable, sig_flag) %>%
  group_by(id, variable) %>%
  summarise(sig_flag = any(sig_flag, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = sig_flag,
    values_fill = list(sig_flag = FALSE)
  )

df_with_significance <- df %>%
  left_join(participant_significance, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    significant_inatt = replace_na(significant_inatt, FALSE),
    significant_hypimp = replace_na(significant_hypimp, FALSE)
  )

# ===================================================================
# 4. Add person-standardized hormone variables
#    ONLY for within-person use later
# ===================================================================
df_with_significance <- df_with_significance %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    E2_mean = mean(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_mean = mean(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_sd = sd(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_sd = sd(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_pz = case_when(
      is.na(E2_sd) | E2_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (E2 - E2_mean) / E2_sd
    ),
    P4_pz = case_when(
      is.na(P4_sd) | P4_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (P4 - P4_mean) / P4_sd
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-E2_mean, -P4_mean, -E2_sd, -P4_sd)

# ===================================================================
# 5. Create subgroup datasets
# ===================================================================
full_sample_df <- df_with_significance

cyclical_inattention_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt)

cyclical_hypimp_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_hypimp)

cyclical_both_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

cyclical_either_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt | significant_hypimp)

inattention_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & !significant_hypimp)

hypimp_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(!significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

group_counts <- tibble(
  group = c(
    "full_sample",
    "cyclical_inattention",
    "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
    "cyclical_both",
    "cyclical_either",
    "inattention_only",
    "hyperactivity_impulsivity_only"
  ),
  n_people = c(
    n_distinct(full_sample_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_inattention_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_hypimp_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_both_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_either_df$id),
    n_distinct(inattention_only_df$id),
    n_distinct(hypimp_only_df$id)
  )
)

print(group_counts)

write_csv(
  group_counts,
  file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("group_counts_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 6. Variable specification for hybrid matrix
#    Display labels stay simple: E2 / P4
#    Between-person + ICC use RAW E2 / P4
#    Within-person uses PERSON-STANDARDIZED E2_pz / P4_pz
# ===================================================================
corr_var_info <- tibble(
  display_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  display_label = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "Inattentive Sx",
    "Hyperactive/Impulsive Sx",
    "Working Memory Deficits (Pinball Task)",
    "Response Disinhibition (Robot Task)",
    "Depression",
    "Anxiety",
    "Irritability",
    "Physical Pain"
  ),
  between_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  within_var = c(
    "E2_pz",
    "P4_pz",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 7. Helper to extract ICC robustly
# ===================================================================
extract_icc_value <- function(icc_out) {

  if (is.null(icc_out)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && !is.null(names(icc_out))) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_adjusted"]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_conditional"]]))
    }
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && is.numeric(icc_out)) {
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.data.frame(icc_out)) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  if (is.list(icc_out)) {
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_adjusted)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_conditional)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  NA_real_
}

# ===================================================================
# 8. Compute hybrid multilevel correlation matrices
# ===================================================================
compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid <- function(data, var_info, id_var) {

  display_vars <- var_info$display_var
  n_vars <- length(display_vars)

  results_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  pval_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Upper triangle = between-person Spearman on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  between_summary_expressions <- purrr::map(
    var_info$between_var,
    ~ rlang::quo(mean(!!rlang::sym(.x), na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
    rlang::set_names(display_vars)

  person_means <- data %>%
    group_by(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    summarise(!!!between_summary_expressions, .groups = "drop")

  if (nrow(person_means) >= 3) {
    between_corr <- suppressWarnings(
      cor(
        person_means[, display_vars],
        method = "spearman",
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
      )
    )

    for (i in 1:(n_vars - 1)) {
      for (j in (i + 1):n_vars) {
        x <- person_means[[display_vars[i]]]
        y <- person_means[[display_vars[j]]]

        if (sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)) > 2) {
          test <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman"))
          results_matrix[i, j] <- between_corr[i, j]
          pval_matrix[i, j] <- test$p.value
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Diagonal = ICC on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in seq_len(n_vars)) {
    raw_var <- var_info$between_var[i]
    display_var <- var_info$display_var[i]

    fml <- as.formula(paste0("`", raw_var, "` ~ 1 + (1|`", id_var, "`)"))

    if (sum(!is.na(data[[raw_var]])) > 1 &&
        dplyr::n_distinct(data[[id_var]][!is.na(data[[raw_var]])]) > 1) {

      model_fit <- try(
        suppressWarnings(lmer(fml, data = data)),
        silent = TRUE
      )

      if (!inherits(model_fit, "try-error")) {
        icc_out <- try(
          suppressWarnings(performance::icc(model_fit)),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(icc_out, "try-error")) {
          icc_val <- extract_icc_value(icc_out)

          if (is.finite(icc_val)) {
            results_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- icc_val
            pval_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- 0
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Lower triangle = within-person rmcorr on person-standardized hormones
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in 2:n_vars) {
    for (j in 1:(i - 1)) {

      var1_display <- display_vars[i]
      var2_display <- display_vars[j]

      var1_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var1_display]
      var2_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var2_display]

      rmcorr_data <- data %>%
        select(
          all_of(id_var),
          x = all_of(var1_within),
          y = all_of(var2_within)
        ) %>%
        drop_na()

      if (nrow(rmcorr_data) > 2 && n_distinct(rmcorr_data[[id_var]]) > 1) {
        rmcorr_result <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id_var,
            measure1 = x,
            measure2 = y,
            dataset = rmcorr_data
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(rmcorr_result, "try-error")) {
          results_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$r
          pval_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$p
        }
      }
    }
  }

  list(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    person_means = person_means
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 9. Convert matrices to long table
# ===================================================================
matrices_to_table <- function(results_matrix, pval_matrix, label_lookup, group_label, n_people) {

  table_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix)) %>%
    rename(var_row = Var1, var_col = Var2, estimate = Freq) %>%
    mutate(
      p_value = as.vector(pval_matrix),
      row_index = match(var_row, rownames(results_matrix)),
      col_index = match(var_col, colnames(results_matrix)),
      cell_type = case_when(
        var_row == var_col ~ "ICC",
        row_index < col_index ~ "Between-person Spearman",
        row_index > col_index ~ "Within-person rmcorr",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      var_row_label = label_lookup[var_row],
      var_col_label = label_lookup[var_col],
      group = group_label,
      n_people = n_people
    ) %>%
    select(group, n_people, cell_type, var_row, var_row_label, var_col, var_col_label, estimate, p_value)

  table_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 10. Heatmap function
# ===================================================================
generate_correlation_heatmap <- function(
    results_matrix,
    pval_matrix,
    final_labels = NULL,
    main_title = "Multilevel Correlation Matrix of Daily Outcomes",
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)",
    sig.level = 0.05,
    label_size = 4.6,
    diag_label_size = 6
) {

  corr_order <- rownames(results_matrix)

  results_matrix_ordered <- results_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]
  pval_matrix_ordered <- pval_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix_ordered))
  names(plot_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "value")
  plot_df$p.value <- as.vector(pval_matrix_ordered)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      row_mat = match(as.character(Var1), corr_order),
      col_mat = match(as.character(Var2), corr_order),
      is_diag = row_mat == col_mat,
      within_person = row_mat > col_mat,
      between_person = row_mat < col_mat
    )

  plot_df$Var1 <- factor(plot_df$Var1, levels = rev(corr_order))
  plot_df$Var2 <- factor(plot_df$Var2, levels = corr_order)

  highlight_df <- plot_df %>%
    filter(
      within_person,
      as.character(Var1) %in% c("E2", "P4") | as.character(Var2) %in% c("E2", "P4")
    )

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      label = no_leading_zero(value),
      is_sig = p.value < sig.level
    )

  diag_df <- plot_df %>% filter(is_diag)
  sig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & is_sig)
  nonsig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & !is_sig)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_tile(
      data = plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag),
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value),
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = "gray92",
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = highlight_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = NA,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 2
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#6D9EC1",
      mid = "white",
      high = "#E46726",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits = c(-1, 1),
      name = "Correlation"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = main_title,
      subtitle = subtitle_text
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank()
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = sig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "black",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = nonsig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      color = "gray40",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "#4B0082",
      size = diag_label_size
    )

  if (!is.null(final_labels)) {
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = final_labels) +
      scale_y_discrete(labels = final_labels)
  }

  return(p)
}

# ===================================================================
# 11. Wrapper for correlation outputs
# ===================================================================
run_and_save_correlation_outputs <- function(
    data,
    group_label,
    corr_var_info,
    output_folder,
    id_var = "id"
) {

  n_people <- data %>%
    distinct(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    nrow()

  message("Running group: ", group_label, " (N = ", n_people, ")")

  if (n_people < 3) {
    warning("Skipping ", group_label, ": fewer than 3 participants.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  label_lookup <- setNames(corr_var_info$display_label, corr_var_info$display_var)

  matrix_results <- compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid(
    data = data,
    var_info = corr_var_info,
    id_var = id_var
  )

  results_matrix <- matrix_results$results_matrix
  pval_matrix <- matrix_results$pval_matrix

  corr_table <- matrices_to_table(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    label_lookup = label_lookup,
    group_label = group_label,
    n_people = n_people
  )

  dated_csv_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_table_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )

  write_csv(corr_table, dated_csv_file)

  heatmap_title <- paste0(
    "Multilevel Correlation Matrix: ",
    group_label,
    " (N = ",
    n_people,
    ")"
  )

  heatmap_plot <- generate_correlation_heatmap(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    final_labels = label_lookup,
    main_title = heatmap_title,
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)"
  )

  dated_png_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_heatmap_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".png")
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = dated_png_file,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    width = 11,
    height = 8.5
  )

  print(heatmap_plot)

  list(
    n_people = n_people,
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    corr_table = corr_table,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    csv_file = dated_csv_file,
    png_file = dated_png_file
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 12. Run correlation outputs
# ===================================================================
full_sample_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = full_sample_df,
  group_label = "full_sample",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_inattention_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_inattention_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_inattention",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_hypimp_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_hypimp_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_both_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_both_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_both",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_either_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_either_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_either",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

# ===================================================================
# 13. Create plotting dataset with cyclical status indicators
# ===================================================================
df_plotmods <- df_with_significance %>%
  mutate(
    cyclical_inattention = if_else(significant_inatt, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity = if_else(significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_both = if_else(significant_inatt & significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_either = if_else(significant_inatt | significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical")
  )

# ===================================================================
# 14. Helper function to prepare scatterplot data
# ===================================================================
prepare_moderation_scatter_data <- function(data, subgroup_var) {

  plot_df <- data %>%
    select(
      id,
      all_of(subgroup_var),
      E2_pz,
      P4_pz,
      CSS_Inatt,
      CSS_HypImp
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(E2_pz, P4_pz),
      names_to = "hormone",
      values_to = "hormone_std"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(CSS_Inatt, CSS_HypImp),
      names_to = "outcome",
      values_to = "symptom"
    ) %>%
    drop_na(hormone_std, symptom)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      subgroup_status = .data[[subgroup_var]],
      subgroup_status = factor(subgroup_status, levels = c("Not Cyclical", "Cyclical")),
      hormone = factor(
        hormone,
        levels = c("E2_pz", "P4_pz"),
        labels = c("Person-standardized E2", "Person-standardized P4")
      ),
      outcome = factor(
        outcome,
        levels = c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp"),
        labels = c("Daily Inattentive Symptoms", "Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms")
      ),
      panel = factor(
        paste(hormone, outcome, sep = " -> "),
        levels = c(
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms"
        )
      )
    )

  plot_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 15. Helper function to count people in each subgroup status
# ===================================================================
get_subgroup_counts <- function(data, subgroup_var) {
  data %>%
    distinct(id, .data[[subgroup_var]]) %>%
    count(.data[[subgroup_var]], name = "n_people") %>%
    complete(
      !!rlang::sym(subgroup_var) := c("Not Cyclical", "Cyclical"),
      fill = list(n_people = 0)
    ) %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(.data[[subgroup_var]], ": N = ", n_people))
}

# ===================================================================
# 16. Helper function to compute within-person rmcorr labels per panel/group
# ===================================================================
compute_panel_rmcorr_labels <- function(plot_df) {

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  combos <- plot_df %>%
    distinct(panel, subgroup_status, hormone, outcome)

  label_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
    list(combos$panel, combos$subgroup_status, combos$hormone, combos$outcome),
    function(panel_i, subgroup_i, hormone_i, outcome_i) {

      dat_i <- plot_df %>%
        filter(
          panel == panel_i,
          subgroup_status == subgroup_i,
          hormone == hormone_i,
          outcome == outcome_i
        ) %>%
        select(id, hormone_std, symptom) %>%
        drop_na()

      n_people <- n_distinct(dat_i$id)

      if (nrow(dat_i) < 3 || n_people < 2) {
        label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
      } else {
        rmc <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id,
            measure1 = hormone_std,
            measure2 = symptom,
            dataset = dat_i
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (inherits(rmc, "try-error")) {
          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
        } else {
          r_txt <- no_leading_zero(rmc$r)

          stars <- case_when(
            rmc$p < .001 ~ "***",
            rmc$p < .01 ~ "**",
            rmc$p < .05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
          )

          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = ", r_txt, stars)
        }
      }

      tibble(
        panel = panel_i,
        subgroup_status = subgroup_i,
        label = label_text,
        x = Inf,
        y = Inf,
        vjust = ifelse(subgroup_i == "Cyclical", 1.2, 2.6)
      )
    }
  )

  label_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 17. Full-sample rmcorr labels
# ===================================================================
compute_fullsample_rmcorr_labels <- function(plot_df) {

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  combos <- plot_df %>%
    distinct(panel, hormone, outcome)

  label_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
    list(combos$panel, combos$hormone, combos$outcome),
    function(panel_i, hormone_i, outcome_i) {

      dat_i <- plot_df %>%
        filter(
          panel == panel_i,
          hormone == hormone_i,
          outcome == outcome_i
        ) %>%
        select(id, hormone_std, symptom) %>%
        drop_na()

      n_people <- n_distinct(dat_i$id)

      if (nrow(dat_i) < 3 || n_people < 2) {
        label_text <- "Full sample: r = NA"
      } else {
        rmc <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id,
            measure1 = hormone_std,
            measure2 = symptom,
            dataset = dat_i
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (inherits(rmc, "try-error")) {
          label_text <- "Full sample: r = NA"
        } else {
          r_txt <- no_leading_zero(rmc$r)

          stars <- case_when(
            rmc$p < .001 ~ "***",
            rmc$p < .01 ~ "**",
            rmc$p < .05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
          )

          label_text <- paste0("Full sample: r = ", r_txt, stars)
        }
      }

      tibble(
        panel = panel_i,
        label = label_text,
        x = Inf,
        y = Inf
      )
    }
  )

  label_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 18. Plotting function for cyclical vs not cyclical moderation
# ===================================================================
plot_hormone_symptom_moderation <- function(
    data,
    subgroup_var,
    subgroup_label,
    output_folder,
    point_alpha = 0.18,
    point_size = 1.2,
    line_size = 1.1
) {

  plot_df <- prepare_moderation_scatter_data(data, subgroup_var)
  subgroup_counts <- get_subgroup_counts(data, subgroup_var)
  label_df <- compute_panel_rmcorr_labels(plot_df)

  count_text <- paste(subgroup_counts$label, collapse = "    |    ")

  p <- ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(x = hormone_std, y = symptom, color = subgroup_status)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = point_alpha, size = point_size) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = line_size) +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = subgroup_status, vjust = vjust),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 1.05,
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ panel,
      nrow = 2,
      scales = "free"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Hormone-Symptom Associations by ", subgroup_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        count_text,
        "\nText in each panel shows within-person repeated-measures correlation (rmcorr)."
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Daily symptom level",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0(
      "scatter_moderation_personstandardized_",
      subgroup_var,
      "_",
      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"),
      ".png"
    )
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 8
  )

  invisible(
    list(
      plot = p,
      plot_data = plot_df,
      label_data = label_df,
      output_file = output_file
    )
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 19. Plotting function for full sample
# ===================================================================
plot_hormone_symptom_fullsample <- function(
    data,
    sample_label,
    output_folder,
    point_alpha = 0.18,
    point_size = 1.2,
    line_size = 1.1
) {

  plot_df <- data %>%
    select(
      id,
      E2_pz,
      P4_pz,
      CSS_Inatt,
      CSS_HypImp
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(E2_pz, P4_pz),
      names_to = "hormone",
      values_to = "hormone_std"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(CSS_Inatt, CSS_HypImp),
      names_to = "outcome",
      values_to = "symptom"
    ) %>%
    drop_na(hormone_std, symptom) %>%
    mutate(
      hormone = factor(
        hormone,
        levels = c("E2_pz", "P4_pz"),
        labels = c("Person-standardized E2", "Person-standardized P4")
      ),
      outcome = factor(
        outcome,
        levels = c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp"),
        labels = c("Daily Inattentive Symptoms", "Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms")
      ),
      panel = factor(
        paste(hormone, outcome, sep = " -> "),
        levels = c(
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms"
        )
      )
    )

  label_df <- compute_fullsample_rmcorr_labels(plot_df)

  n_people <- n_distinct(plot_df$id)

  p <- ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(x = hormone_std, y = symptom)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = point_alpha, size = point_size, color = "gray35") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = line_size, color = "black") +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 1.05,
      vjust = 1.2,
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ panel,
      nrow = 2,
      scales = "free"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Hormone-Symptom Associations: ", sample_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Full sample: N = ",
        n_people,
        "\nText in each panel shows within-person repeated-measures correlation (rmcorr)."
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Daily symptom level"
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0(
      "scatter_fullsample_personstandardized_",
      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"),
      ".png"
    )
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 8
  )

  invisible(
    list(
      plot = p,
      plot_data = plot_df,
      label_data = label_df,
      output_file = output_file
    )
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 20. Run scatterplots
# ===================================================================
scatter_fullsample_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_fullsample(
  data = df_plotmods,
  sample_label = "Full Sample",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_inattention_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_inattention",
  subgroup_label = "Cyclical Inattention",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_hypimp_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
  subgroup_label = "Cyclical Hyperactivity/Impulsivity",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_both_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_both",
  subgroup_label = "Both Cyclical Inattention and Cyclical Hyperactivity/Impulsivity",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_either_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_either",
  subgroup_label = "Either Cyclical Inattention or Cyclical Hyperactivity/Impulsivity",
  output_folder = output_folder
)
```

# Select people with valid hormone data first

```{r}
# ===================================================================
# 1. Load Required Libraries
# ===================================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(rmcorr)
library(performance)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)

# ===================================================================
# 2. Load participant-level GAM significance summary
# ===================================================================
singlegaminfo <- read_csv(
  "/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_code_dataedits_output/5_singlegams_output/20250923_Participant_Reports_PDF_byID/participant_significance_summary_20250923.csv"
)

# ===================================================================
# 3. Harmonize ID type and create participant-level cyclical flags
# ===================================================================
df <- df %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id))

participant_significance <- singlegaminfo %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(id)) %>%
  filter(variable %in% c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp")) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_flag = p.value < 0.05,
    variable = recode(
      variable,
      "CSS_Inatt" = "significant_inatt",
      "CSS_HypImp" = "significant_hypimp"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(id, variable, sig_flag) %>%
  group_by(id, variable) %>%
  summarise(sig_flag = any(sig_flag, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = sig_flag,
    values_fill = list(sig_flag = FALSE)
  )

df_with_significance <- df %>%
  left_join(participant_significance, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    significant_inatt = replace_na(significant_inatt, FALSE),
    significant_hypimp = replace_na(significant_hypimp, FALSE)
  )

# ===================================================================
# 4. Restrict to analytic sample with any available hormone data
#    This is the shared sample for ALL tables, heatmaps, and scatterplots
# ===================================================================
analytic_ids_with_hormones <- df_with_significance %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    has_any_hormone_data = any(!is.na(E2) | !is.na(P4)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(has_any_hormone_data) %>%
  pull(id)

df_with_significance <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(id %in% analytic_ids_with_hormones)

# ===================================================================
# 5. Add person-standardized hormone variables
#    ONLY for within-person use later
# ===================================================================
df_with_significance <- df_with_significance %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    E2_mean = mean(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_mean = mean(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_sd = sd(E2, na.rm = TRUE),
    P4_sd = sd(P4, na.rm = TRUE),
    E2_pz = case_when(
      is.na(E2_sd) | E2_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (E2 - E2_mean) / E2_sd
    ),
    P4_pz = case_when(
      is.na(P4_sd) | P4_sd == 0 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ (P4 - P4_mean) / P4_sd
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-E2_mean, -P4_mean, -E2_sd, -P4_sd)

# ===================================================================
# 6. Create subgroup datasets
# ===================================================================
full_sample_df <- df_with_significance

cyclical_inattention_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt)

cyclical_hypimp_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_hypimp)

cyclical_both_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

cyclical_either_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt | significant_hypimp)

inattention_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(significant_inatt & !significant_hypimp)

hypimp_only_df <- df_with_significance %>%
  filter(!significant_inatt & significant_hypimp)

group_counts <- tibble(
  group = c(
    "full_sample",
    "cyclical_inattention",
    "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
    "cyclical_both",
    "cyclical_either",
    "inattention_only",
    "hyperactivity_impulsivity_only"
  ),
  n_people = c(
    n_distinct(full_sample_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_inattention_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_hypimp_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_both_df$id),
    n_distinct(cyclical_either_df$id),
    n_distinct(inattention_only_df$id),
    n_distinct(hypimp_only_df$id)
  )
)

print(group_counts)

write_csv(
  group_counts,
  file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("group_counts_hormone_analytic_sample_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 7. Variable specification for hybrid matrix
#    Display labels stay simple: E2 / P4
#    Between-person + ICC use RAW E2 / P4
#    Within-person uses PERSON-STANDARDIZED E2_pz / P4_pz
# ===================================================================
corr_var_info <- tibble(
  display_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  display_label = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "Inattentive Sx",
    "Hyperactive/Impulsive Sx",
    "Working Memory Deficits (Pinball Task)",
    "Response Disinhibition (Robot Task)",
    "Depression",
    "Anxiety",
    "Irritability",
    "Physical Pain"
  ),
  between_var = c(
    "E2",
    "P4",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  ),
  within_var = c(
    "E2_pz",
    "P4_pz",
    "CSS_Inatt",
    "CSS_HypImp",
    "score_pinball_rev",
    "score_robot_rev",
    "DRSP_1",
    "DRSP_4",
    "DRSP_7",
    "DRSP_21"
  )
)

# ===================================================================
# 8. Helper to extract ICC robustly
# ===================================================================
extract_icc_value <- function(icc_out) {

  if (is.null(icc_out)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && !is.null(names(icc_out))) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_adjusted"]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out[["ICC_conditional"]]))
    }
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.atomic(icc_out) && is.numeric(icc_out)) {
    return(as.numeric(icc_out[[1]]))
  }

  if (is.data.frame(icc_out)) {
    if ("ICC_adjusted" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if ("ICC_conditional" %in% names(icc_out)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  if (is.list(icc_out)) {
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_adjusted)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_adjusted[[1]]))
    }
    if (!is.null(icc_out$ICC_conditional)) {
      return(as.numeric(icc_out$ICC_conditional[[1]]))
    }
  }

  NA_real_
}

# ===================================================================
# 9. Compute hybrid multilevel correlation matrices
# ===================================================================
compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid <- function(data, var_info, id_var) {

  display_vars <- var_info$display_var
  n_vars <- length(display_vars)

  results_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  pval_matrix <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = n_vars,
    ncol = n_vars,
    dimnames = list(display_vars, display_vars)
  )

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Upper triangle = between-person Spearman on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  between_summary_expressions <- purrr::map(
    var_info$between_var,
    ~ rlang::quo(mean(!!rlang::sym(.x), na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
    rlang::set_names(display_vars)

  person_means <- data %>%
    group_by(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    summarise(!!!between_summary_expressions, .groups = "drop")

  if (nrow(person_means) >= 3) {
    between_corr <- suppressWarnings(
      cor(
        person_means[, display_vars],
        method = "spearman",
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
      )
    )

    for (i in 1:(n_vars - 1)) {
      for (j in (i + 1):n_vars) {
        x <- person_means[[display_vars[i]]]
        y <- person_means[[display_vars[j]]]

        if (sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)) > 2) {
          test <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman"))
          results_matrix[i, j] <- between_corr[i, j]
          pval_matrix[i, j] <- test$p.value
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Diagonal = ICC on RAW variables
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in seq_len(n_vars)) {
    raw_var <- var_info$between_var[i]
    display_var <- var_info$display_var[i]

    fml <- as.formula(paste0("`", raw_var, "` ~ 1 + (1|`", id_var, "`)"))

    if (sum(!is.na(data[[raw_var]])) > 1 &&
        dplyr::n_distinct(data[[id_var]][!is.na(data[[raw_var]])]) > 1) {

      model_fit <- try(
        suppressWarnings(lmer(fml, data = data)),
        silent = TRUE
      )

      if (!inherits(model_fit, "try-error")) {
        icc_out <- try(
          suppressWarnings(performance::icc(model_fit)),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(icc_out, "try-error")) {
          icc_val <- extract_icc_value(icc_out)

          if (is.finite(icc_val)) {
            results_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- icc_val
            pval_matrix[display_var, display_var] <- 0
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Lower triangle = within-person rmcorr on person-standardized hormones
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  for (i in 2:n_vars) {
    for (j in 1:(i - 1)) {

      var1_display <- display_vars[i]
      var2_display <- display_vars[j]

      var1_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var1_display]
      var2_within <- var_info$within_var[var_info$display_var == var2_display]

      rmcorr_data <- data %>%
        select(
          all_of(id_var),
          x = all_of(var1_within),
          y = all_of(var2_within)
        ) %>%
        drop_na()

      if (nrow(rmcorr_data) > 2 && n_distinct(rmcorr_data[[id_var]]) > 1) {
        rmcorr_result <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id_var,
            measure1 = x,
            measure2 = y,
            dataset = rmcorr_data
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (!inherits(rmcorr_result, "try-error")) {
          results_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$r
          pval_matrix[var1_display, var2_display] <- rmcorr_result$p
        }
      }
    }
  }

  list(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    person_means = person_means
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 10. Convert matrices to long table
# ===================================================================
```
