```{r}
matrices_to_table <- function(results_matrix, pval_matrix, label_lookup, group_label, n_people) {

  table_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix)) %>%
    rename(var_row = Var1, var_col = Var2, estimate = Freq) %>%
    mutate(
      p_value = as.vector(pval_matrix),
      row_index = match(var_row, rownames(results_matrix)),
      col_index = match(var_col, colnames(results_matrix)),
      cell_type = case_when(
        var_row == var_col ~ "ICC",
        row_index < col_index ~ "Between-person Spearman",
        row_index > col_index ~ "Within-person rmcorr",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      var_row_label = label_lookup[var_row],
      var_col_label = label_lookup[var_col],
      group = group_label,
      n_people = n_people
    ) %>%
    select(group, n_people, cell_type, var_row, var_row_label, var_col, var_col_label, estimate, p_value)

  table_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 11. Heatmap function
# ===================================================================
generate_correlation_heatmap <- function(
    results_matrix,
    pval_matrix,
    final_labels = NULL,
    main_title = "Multilevel Correlation Matrix of Daily Outcomes",
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)",
    sig.level = 0.05,
    label_size = 4.6,
    diag_label_size = 6
) {

  corr_order <- rownames(results_matrix)

  results_matrix_ordered <- results_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]
  pval_matrix_ordered <- pval_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix_ordered))
  names(plot_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "value")
  plot_df$p.value <- as.vector(pval_matrix_ordered)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      row_mat = match(as.character(Var1), corr_order),
      col_mat = match(as.character(Var2), corr_order),
      is_diag = row_mat == col_mat,
      within_person = row_mat > col_mat,
      between_person = row_mat < col_mat
    )

  plot_df$Var1 <- factor(plot_df$Var1, levels = rev(corr_order))
  plot_df$Var2 <- factor(plot_df$Var2, levels = corr_order)

  highlight_df <- plot_df %>%
    filter(
      within_person,
      as.character(Var1) %in% c("E2", "P4") | as.character(Var2) %in% c("E2", "P4")
    )

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      label = no_leading_zero(value),
      is_sig = p.value < sig.level
    )

  diag_df <- plot_df %>% filter(is_diag)
  sig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & is_sig)
  nonsig_df <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & !is_sig)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_tile(
      data = plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag),
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value),
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = "gray92",
      color = "gray70",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_tile(
      data = highlight_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
      fill = NA,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 2
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#6D9EC1",
      mid = "white",
      high = "#E46726",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits = c(-1, 1),
      name = "Correlation"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = main_title,
      subtitle = subtitle_text
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank()
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = sig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "black",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = nonsig_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      color = "gray40",
      size = label_size
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = diag_df,
      aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
      fontface = "bold",
      color = "#4B0082",
      size = diag_label_size
    )

  if (!is.null(final_labels)) {
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = final_labels) +
      scale_y_discrete(labels = final_labels)
  }

  return(p)
}

# ===================================================================
# 12. Wrapper for correlation outputs
# ===================================================================
run_and_save_correlation_outputs <- function(
    data,
    group_label,
    corr_var_info,
    output_folder,
    id_var = "id"
) {

  n_people <- n_distinct(data[[id_var]])

  message("Running group: ", group_label, " (N = ", n_people, ")")

  if (n_people < 3) {
    warning("Skipping ", group_label, ": fewer than 3 participants.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  label_lookup <- setNames(corr_var_info$display_label, corr_var_info$display_var)

  matrix_results <- compute_multilevel_correlation_matrices_hybrid(
    data = data,
    var_info = corr_var_info,
    id_var = id_var
  )

  results_matrix <- matrix_results$results_matrix
  pval_matrix <- matrix_results$pval_matrix

  corr_table <- matrices_to_table(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    label_lookup = label_lookup,
    group_label = group_label,
    n_people = n_people
  )

  dated_csv_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_table_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
  )

  write_csv(corr_table, dated_csv_file)

  heatmap_title <- paste0(
    "Multilevel Correlation Matrix: ",
    group_label,
    " (N = ",
    n_people,
    ")"
  )

  heatmap_plot <- generate_correlation_heatmap(
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    final_labels = label_lookup,
    main_title = heatmap_title,
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Between-person (Spearman, raw hormones) | Lower: Within-person (rmcorr, person-standardized hormones) | Diagonal: ICC (raw hormones)"
  )

  dated_png_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("corr_heatmap_hybridHormones_", group_label, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".png")
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = dated_png_file,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    width = 11,
    height = 8.5
  )

  print(heatmap_plot)

  list(
    n_people = n_people,
    results_matrix = results_matrix,
    pval_matrix = pval_matrix,
    corr_table = corr_table,
    plot = heatmap_plot,
    csv_file = dated_csv_file,
    png_file = dated_png_file
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 13. Run correlation outputs
# ===================================================================
full_sample_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = full_sample_df,
  group_label = "full_sample",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_inattention_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_inattention_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_inattention",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_hypimp_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_hypimp_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_both_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_both_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_both",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

cyclical_either_results_hybrid <- run_and_save_correlation_outputs(
  data = cyclical_either_df,
  group_label = "cyclical_either",
  corr_var_info = corr_var_info,
  output_folder = output_folder,
  id_var = "id"
)

# ===================================================================
# 14. Create plotting dataset with cyclical status indicators
# ===================================================================
df_plotmods <- df_with_significance %>%
  mutate(
    cyclical_inattention = if_else(significant_inatt, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity = if_else(significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_both = if_else(significant_inatt & significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical"),
    cyclical_either = if_else(significant_inatt | significant_hypimp, "Cyclical", "Not Cyclical")
  )

# ===================================================================
# 15. Helper function to prepare scatterplot data
# ===================================================================
prepare_moderation_scatter_data <- function(data, subgroup_var) {

  plot_df <- data %>%
    select(
      id,
      all_of(subgroup_var),
      E2_pz,
      P4_pz,
      CSS_Inatt,
      CSS_HypImp
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(E2_pz, P4_pz),
      names_to = "hormone",
      values_to = "hormone_std"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(CSS_Inatt, CSS_HypImp),
      names_to = "outcome",
      values_to = "symptom"
    ) %>%
    drop_na(hormone_std, symptom)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      subgroup_status = .data[[subgroup_var]],
      subgroup_status = factor(subgroup_status, levels = c("Not Cyclical", "Cyclical")),
      hormone = factor(
        hormone,
        levels = c("E2_pz", "P4_pz"),
        labels = c("Person-standardized E2", "Person-standardized P4")
      ),
      outcome = factor(
        outcome,
        levels = c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp"),
        labels = c("Daily Inattentive Symptoms", "Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms")
      ),
      panel = factor(
        paste(hormone, outcome, sep = " -> "),
        levels = c(
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms"
        )
      )
    )

  plot_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 16. Helper function to count people in each subgroup status
# ===================================================================
get_subgroup_counts <- function(data, subgroup_var) {
  data %>%
    distinct(id, .data[[subgroup_var]]) %>%
    count(.data[[subgroup_var]], name = "n_people") %>%
    complete(
      !!rlang::sym(subgroup_var) := c("Not Cyclical", "Cyclical"),
      fill = list(n_people = 0)
    ) %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(.data[[subgroup_var]], ": N = ", n_people))
}

# ===================================================================
# 17. Helper function to compute within-person rmcorr labels per panel/group
# ===================================================================
compute_panel_rmcorr_labels <- function(plot_df) {

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  combos <- plot_df %>%
    distinct(panel, subgroup_status, hormone, outcome)

  label_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
    list(combos$panel, combos$subgroup_status, combos$hormone, combos$outcome),
    function(panel_i, subgroup_i, hormone_i, outcome_i) {

      dat_i <- plot_df %>%
        filter(
          panel == panel_i,
          subgroup_status == subgroup_i,
          hormone == hormone_i,
          outcome == outcome_i
        ) %>%
        select(id, hormone_std, symptom) %>%
        drop_na()

      n_people <- n_distinct(dat_i$id)

      if (nrow(dat_i) < 3 || n_people < 2) {
        label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
      } else {
        rmc <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id,
            measure1 = hormone_std,
            measure2 = symptom,
            dataset = dat_i
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (inherits(rmc, "try-error")) {
          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = NA")
        } else {
          r_txt <- no_leading_zero(rmc$r)

          stars <- case_when(
            rmc$p < .001 ~ "***",
            rmc$p < .01 ~ "**",
            rmc$p < .05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
          )

          label_text <- paste0(subgroup_i, ": r = ", r_txt, stars)
        }
      }

      tibble(
        panel = panel_i,
        subgroup_status = subgroup_i,
        label = label_text,
        x = Inf,
        y = Inf,
        vjust = ifelse(subgroup_i == "Cyclical", 1.2, 2.6)
      )
    }
  )

  label_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 18. Full-sample rmcorr labels
# ===================================================================
compute_fullsample_rmcorr_labels <- function(plot_df) {

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(x),
      NA_character_,
      sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x))
    )
  }

  combos <- plot_df %>%
    distinct(panel, hormone, outcome)

  label_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
    list(combos$panel, combos$hormone, combos$outcome),
    function(panel_i, hormone_i, outcome_i) {

      dat_i <- plot_df %>%
        filter(
          panel == panel_i,
          hormone == hormone_i,
          outcome == outcome_i
        ) %>%
        select(id, hormone_std, symptom) %>%
        drop_na()

      n_people <- n_distinct(dat_i$id)

      if (nrow(dat_i) < 3 || n_people < 2) {
        label_text <- "Full sample: r = NA"
      } else {
        rmc <- try(
          rmcorr::rmcorr(
            participant = id,
            measure1 = hormone_std,
            measure2 = symptom,
            dataset = dat_i
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )

        if (inherits(rmc, "try-error")) {
          label_text <- "Full sample: r = NA"
        } else {
          r_txt <- no_leading_zero(rmc$r)

          stars <- case_when(
            rmc$p < .001 ~ "***",
            rmc$p < .01 ~ "**",
            rmc$p < .05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
          )

          label_text <- paste0("Full sample: r = ", r_txt, stars)
        }
      }

      tibble(
        panel = panel_i,
        label = label_text,
        x = Inf,
        y = Inf
      )
    }
  )

  label_df
}

# ===================================================================
# 19. Plotting function for cyclical vs not cyclical moderation
# ===================================================================
plot_hormone_symptom_moderation <- function(
    data,
    subgroup_var,
    subgroup_label,
    output_folder,
    point_alpha = 0.18,
    point_size = 1.2,
    line_size = 1.1
) {

  plot_df <- prepare_moderation_scatter_data(data, subgroup_var)
  subgroup_counts <- get_subgroup_counts(data, subgroup_var)
  label_df <- compute_panel_rmcorr_labels(plot_df)

  count_text <- paste(subgroup_counts$label, collapse = "    |    ")

  p <- ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(x = hormone_std, y = symptom, color = subgroup_status)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = point_alpha, size = point_size) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = line_size) +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = subgroup_status, vjust = vjust),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 1.05,
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ panel,
      nrow = 2,
      scales = "free"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Hormone-Symptom Associations by ", subgroup_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        count_text,
        "\nText in each panel shows within-person repeated-measures correlation (rmcorr)."
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Daily symptom level",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0(
      "scatter_moderation_personstandardized_",
      subgroup_var,
      "_",
      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"),
      ".png"
    )
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 8
  )

  invisible(
    list(
      plot = p,
      plot_data = plot_df,
      label_data = label_df,
      output_file = output_file
    )
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 20. Plotting function for full sample
# ===================================================================
plot_hormone_symptom_fullsample <- function(
    data,
    sample_label,
    output_folder,
    point_alpha = 0.18,
    point_size = 1.2,
    line_size = 1.1
) {

  plot_df <- data %>%
    select(
      id,
      E2_pz,
      P4_pz,
      CSS_Inatt,
      CSS_HypImp
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(E2_pz, P4_pz),
      names_to = "hormone",
      values_to = "hormone_std"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(CSS_Inatt, CSS_HypImp),
      names_to = "outcome",
      values_to = "symptom"
    ) %>%
    drop_na(hormone_std, symptom) %>%
    mutate(
      hormone = factor(
        hormone,
        levels = c("E2_pz", "P4_pz"),
        labels = c("Person-standardized E2", "Person-standardized P4")
      ),
      outcome = factor(
        outcome,
        levels = c("CSS_Inatt", "CSS_HypImp"),
        labels = c("Daily Inattentive Symptoms", "Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms")
      ),
      panel = factor(
        paste(hormone, outcome, sep = " -> "),
        levels = c(
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized E2 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Inattentive Symptoms",
          "Person-standardized P4 -> Daily Hyperactive/Impulsive Symptoms"
        )
      )
    )

  label_df <- compute_fullsample_rmcorr_labels(plot_df)

  n_people <- n_distinct(plot_df$id)

  p <- ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(x = hormone_std, y = symptom)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = point_alpha, size = point_size, color = "gray35") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = line_size, color = "black") +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 1.05,
      vjust = 1.2,
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ panel,
      nrow = 2,
      scales = "free"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Hormone-Symptom Associations: ", sample_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Full sample: N = ",
        n_people,
        "\nText in each panel shows within-person repeated-measures correlation (rmcorr)."
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Daily symptom level"
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0(
      "scatter_fullsample_personstandardized_",
      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"),
      ".png"
    )
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 8
  )

  invisible(
    list(
      plot = p,
      plot_data = plot_df,
      label_data = label_df,
      output_file = output_file
    )
  )
}

# ===================================================================
# 21. Run scatterplots
# ===================================================================
scatter_fullsample_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_fullsample(
  data = df_plotmods,
  sample_label = "Full Sample",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_inattention_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_inattention",
  subgroup_label = "Cyclical Inattention",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_hypimp_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_hyperactivity_impulsivity",
  subgroup_label = "Cyclical Hyperactivity/Impulsivity",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_both_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_both",
  subgroup_label = "Both Cyclical Inattention and Cyclical Hyperactivity/Impulsivity",
  output_folder = output_folder
)

scatter_cyclical_either_pz <- plot_hormone_symptom_moderation(
  data = df_plotmods,
  subgroup_var = "cyclical_either",
  subgroup_label = "Cyclical ADHD Sx (Inattention or Hyperactivity/Impulsivity)",
  output_folder = output_folder
)
```
